prompt: I want to design a SaaS product login page; give me 3 stylistic directions to compare side by side
expected: Trigger clarifying questions about design context/brand; produce 3 design_canvas variations; avoid AI slop (no purple gradients, no emoji, no Inter); provide concrete reasoning for the dimensions on which each variation differs
tests: Workflow asks questions + variation logic + anti-AI-slop checklist + design_canvas usage

prompt: Help me make a 10-page product pitch deck about an AI tool startup project
expected: Use deck_stage.js as the starting point; first verbally vocalize the design system (color/typography/layout rhythm) and confirm it; alternate between section divider/content/data/quote layouts; font size >= 24px; 1-indexed labels
tests: Junior Designer reports first then executes + deck_stage usage + visual rhythm + scale conventions

prompt: Make a 30-second HTML animation explaining how neural networks work
expected: Use the Stage + Sprite from animations.jsx; write the timeline before the components; easeOut on entry, easeIn on exit; tell the story in phases instead of piling on animations; text dwells for >= 3 seconds
tests: animations workflow + correct easing + rhythm design + duration control

prompt: Build a Habit Tracker App prototype
expected: Ask the user whether they want an overview tile-up or a flow demo (default to overview); use assets/ios_frame.jsx, do not hand-write the Dynamic Island; a Tracker is a high-density type, so each screen needs >= 3 information-density elements (habit completion rate, streak count, trend curves, achievement badges, etc., not decorative ones); at least 5-7 screens side-by-side (home / new habit / detail / stats / settings)
tests: overview/flow form routing + ios_frame hard binding + information-density classification (high-density type) + multiple screens side-by-side

expected: Overview tile-up by default; ios_frame.jsx; reading notes lean toward the content-display category, so the information-density requirement is not as extreme as a Tracker, but the notes list page still needs >= 3 layers of information (book, quote, tags, progress); at least 4-6 screens (home bookshelf / note detail / annotation highlight / search / notebook management); prefer a serif display font
tests: overview by default + ios_frame + information hierarchy + content-led visual rhythm

expected: Overview tile-up; ios_frame.jsx; a running app is a high-density type (map, pace curve, heart-rate zones, per-kilometer split data) and each screen needs >= 3 product-differentiating pieces of information; at least 5 screens (today's overview / live in-run data / route map / history / monthly stats); avoid AI slop (no purple gradients, no piles of decorative icons, but data-visualization icons are allowed)
tests: overview + ios_frame + high-density-type data visualization + map/chart mixed layout + slop boundary conditions
